description: A client retry after a timeout re-applies a non-idempotent operation, doubling its effect because the first attempt actually succeeded.
body: Failure mode: a mutating request succeeds on the server but the acknowledgement is lost, the client times out and retries, and without a dedup key the server applies the operation a second time, double-charging or double-incrementing. Detection signals: duplicate effects correlated with elevated latency or packet loss; counts higher than client request counts; no idempotency key on mutating endpoints. Do not treat a timeout as proof the operation did not happen. Safer fallback: attach a stable idempotency key to every mutation, persist applied keys, and return the original result on a duplicate so the effect occurs exactly once despite at-least-once delivery.
